commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Hebrew, "Chomarim "each of contained 100 gomers. One gomer was the daily allowance of manna for each person, and of course there must have been sufficient quails for one hundred days. But Moses tells us that each one collected at least ten times that quantity, or as much has he could eat for 1,000 days. Bo chart therefore supposes, that only each family, of ten people, gathered so much: or the Hebrew should be rendered heaps, as the core, or chomer, is not a proper measure for birds, but for corn and liquors. The Septuagint, Syriac, have "heaps. "We need not have recourse to a new creation of these birds, as their numbers are very surprising. ) In Italy above 100,000 have been caught in one day, within the space of 5,000 paces. ) The Psalmist compares the numbers brought on this occasion, to the dust, or to the sand of the sea-shore, Psalm lxxvii. Dried them in the sun, having first salted them, as the Egyptians did. (Calmet) (Athenæus. ) Many quails are found in Egypt, and around the Arabian Gulf. (Josephus, iii. ) (Du Hamel).

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
